title: artemisia campestris var. glutinosa
intro: Artemisia campestris var. glutinosa, also known as Field Sagewort or Sticky Sage, is a perennial plant belonging to the Asteraceae family, characterized by its distinctive sticky leaves and a strong, pungent odor. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, which help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. The health benefits of Field Sagewort include its potential to aid in the treatment of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections, as well as its ability to support the immune system and reduce inflammation. Field Sagewort can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often used in herbal remedies for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties.
